Solution:

\text{Let } \alpha = \csc^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{3} \, \sqrt{10}\right) \text{ and } \beta = \csc^{-1}\left(\sqrt{10}\right).

\text{Based on principal domains, } \alpha \text{ is in Quadrant } I \text{ and } \beta \text{ is in Quadrant } I.

\text{Using the reference triangles, we identify the sides:}

\text{For } \alpha: x = 3, \quad y = 9, \quad r = 3 \, \sqrt{10}

\text{For } \beta: x = 9, \quad y = 3, \quad r = 3 \, \sqrt{10}

\text{Evaluate the fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{3}{10} \, \sqrt{10}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{1}{10} \, \sqrt{10}

\sin(\beta) = \frac{1}{10} \, \sqrt{10}, \quad \cos(\beta) = \frac{3}{10} \, \sqrt{10}

\text{Apply the sum/difference identity:}

\sin(\alpha + \beta) = \sin(\alpha)\cos(\beta) + \cos(\alpha)\sin(\beta)

\sin(\alpha + \beta) = \left(\frac{3}{10} \, \sqrt{10}\right)\left(\frac{3}{10} \, \sqrt{10}\right) + \left(\frac{1}{10} \, \sqrt{10}\right)\left(\frac{1}{10} \, \sqrt{10}\right)

\sin(\alpha + \beta) = 1
